approval_note: User approved implementing Zibaldone-style in-app updates plus a general settings view (update/theme) for the TQR desktop app, with the TQR-specific constraint that the browser bundle stays loopback-only (all update traffic is Rust-side), and the version bump 0.1.9 -> 0.2.0.

changes.updater_core: frontend/src-tauri/Cargo.toml adds tauri-plugin-updater = "2"; frontend/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json adds plugins.updater (minisign pubkey + anonymous https://api.github.com/repos/justinyu73/tw-quant-research/releases/latest endpoint); frontend/src-tauri/src/lib.rs registers the plugin and adds check_app_update (returns update availability/version/notes as JSON) and install_app_update (stops the bundled sidecar first so the installer can replace it on Windows, revives the sidecar on failure, app.restart() on success). Sidecar spawn/kill logic refactored into spawn_sidecar/stop_sidecar/revive_sidecar with SidecarConfig state.
changes.settings_view: ui/dashboard/dashboard-core.js adds the settings section; ui/dashboard/app.js adds the settings view — update card (current version via __TAURI__.app.getVersion, check/install buttons, browser preview shows 瀏覽器預覽不提供更新) and theme card (light/dark via [data-theme="dark"] token overrides in ui/dashboard/styles.css, choice persisted in localStorage tw-quant-engine-theme.v1, applied at startup). scripts/dashboard-rwd-audit.cjs covers the settings view.
changes.release_workflow: .github/workflows/desktop-release.yml — build step passes TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY/PASSWORD secrets so nsis .exe.sig and .app.tar.gz.sig are emitted; release asset collection keeps updater payloads and .sig sidecars with per-platform friendly names; new step assembles latest.json (windows-x86_64, darwin-x86_64, darwin-aarch64 asset API URLs + signatures) and uploads it to the draft release; release notes document the updater. audit/strict/public-tree/export gates untouched.
changes.docs: docs/desktop-release.md gains an In-app updates section (mechanism, signing secrets, latest.json, desktop-only); docs/open-source-release-scope.md notes the update check does not change the loopback-only boundary.
changes.smoke_baselines: scripts/dashboard-browser-smoke.cjs EXPECTED_SCREENSHOTS updated after manual review of all affected PNGs (new settings nav entry changed every view) plus settings light/dark 1440px/390px captures.

zibaldone_differences: Zibaldone resolves the GitHub release in the frontend and passes endpoint+token into Rust; TQR does everything Rust-side via tauri.conf.json endpoints because the TQR browser bundle must stay loopback-only (p4_research_closure browser_loopback_only). No token: the repo is public and access is anonymous.
Zibaldone kills a differently-named sidecar before install; TQR stops/revives its own managed tqe-sidecar child via the existing SidecarProcess state.
No Zibaldone comment text was copied; all Rust sources remain free of the forbidden order substring.

github_secrets_todo: TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY_PASSWORD = key password (handed to the repo owner out of band)

cargo_check.result: Finished dev profile; tauri-plugin-updater compiles clean

open_source_audit_strict.result: status pass, strict true; no secret leakage (pubkey only)

workflow_yaml.result: parses; release job has the latest.json step; build step carries both signing env vars

dashboard_rwd_audit.result: 10 views (settings included) x 6 breakpoints, 0 errors; only the intentional 720px breadcrumb clip per view; no non-breadcrumb offenders

acceptance_notes: Done: updater core (Rust-side check/install against the public GitHub release), settings view with update and theme cards, dark theme token overrides, 0.2.0 version alignment (tauri.conf.json + Cargo.toml; the two package.json files carry no version field), signing keypair generated with the private key outside the repo, release workflow signing + latest.json assembly, docs.

Not done: real-machine update verification — it needs a signed v0.2.0+ release published with the new GitHub secrets, then installing an older build and updating through Settings; that is the human acceptance gate, so this stays implemented_pending_human_acceptance.
Known limits: the K-line canvas keeps its default palette in dark mode (noted in the theme card copy); the dark theme is a token-level override of the terminal surfaces, not a full re-skin.

Boundary unchanged: the browser bundle never contacts GitHub; update traffic is Rust-side, anonymous, user-triggered; no provider calls, no order surface, loopback-only research surface; open_source_audit --strict passes.
